records.ety: [L. extricatus, p. p. of extricare to extricate; ex out + tricae trifles, impediments, perplexities. Cf. Intricate.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Extricated(ks"tr*kā`td); p. pr. & vb. n. Extricating(ks"tr*kā`tng).]

defs: To free, as from difficulties or perplexities; to disentangle; to disembarrass; as, to extricate a person from debt, peril, etc.
quotes: We had now extricated ourselves from the various labyrinths and defiles.

defs: To cause to be emitted or evolved; as, to extricate heat or moisture.
